RaceName: Best Ticket Deals Online leicester-racecourse.co.uk Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Closest finish at Wetherby (7f, good to firm; helped to set pace) on second start, before fourth again when 25-1 again on Newcastle AW (7f); greater market interest might signal she has a chance on this nursery debut.

RaceName: Best Ticket Deals Online leicester-racecourse.co.uk Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Six races, runner-up in her only two on AW; improved efforts when blinkered by new yard for last two starts, close second in a maiden at Newcastle latest on the step up to 7f; needs better again on this nursery debut.

RaceName: Best Ticket Deals Online leicester-racecourse.co.uk Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Upped markedly in trip with cheekpieces added for nursery debut at Beverley (7.4f, good to firm) ten days ago and justified 11-10 favouritism in five-runner race by leading on the post; Tiger In The Tree nearly 2l back in third; up 3lb and bare 7f may be on the short side but this should be a weak field.

RaceName: Best Ticket Deals Online leicester-racecourse.co.uk Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Finished a bit closer in each of his three runs, albeit still beaten about 7l when fifth of nine in C&D maiden (good; prominent long way at 66-1) last time; gelded soon afterwards and now wears cheekpieces; needs improvement on this handicap debut.

RaceName: Best Ticket Deals Online leicester-racecourse.co.uk Nursery Handicap
OwnerName: D Kilburn, C Wright, F Mahony & Partner
Spotlight: Low-level form on all four starts, 33-1 for latest on her nursery debut on Kempton AW (7f, AW); needs to show something very new.

RaceName: Best Ticket Deals Online leicester-racecourse.co.uk Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Late foal who's been never dangerous in her three starts, all over 5f; dam was a 2yo 7f winner (RPR 84) and this daughter could be much more interesting now trying that trip on nursery debut; worth a market check.

RaceName: Best Ticket Deals Online leicester-racecourse.co.uk Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Last on his first two starts (6f; bit slipped on first occasion) but beating one home in a six-runner maiden at Hamilton (5f, good to soft; 50-1) four weeks ago was a first sign of significant ability; gelded since and makes nursery debut over 7f, which looks much more the ticket on pedigree.

RaceName: Best Ticket Deals Online leicester-racecourse.co.uk Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Best when fifth of eight at 100-1 for nursery debut at Kempton (upped from 5f to 7f, AW) 12 days ago, having hung badly left turning for home; cheekpieces (worn on penultimate start) return; another 5lb claimer takes over; capable of better if keeping far more of a straight line.

RaceName: Best Ticket Deals Online leicester-racecourse.co.uk Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Twice showed a bit of ability from her four starts, notably on latest when keeping on from the back to be about 2l third of five to Emerald Bay on nursery debut at Beverley (7.4f, good to firm); dropped just 1lb but 5lb claimer and cheekpieces are enlisted; not entirely dismissed.

RaceName: Best Ticket Deals Online leicester-racecourse.co.uk Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Tongue tied for nursery debut at Chepstow (6f, good to firm; 12-1) on latest start and it was her closest finish, albeit beaten about 6l in last of seven under Olivia Tubb; blinkered this time; others have more substantial claims.

RaceName: ITBA Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Point-to-point winner; 0-14 over hurdles but loads of placed efforts to her name, including off 92 in a C&D handicap on penultimate; should go well but rated 95 and likely vulnerable to less exposed rivals; new headgear combination tried.

RaceName: ITBA Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Point-to-point winner; not beaten far when third in a mares' maiden hurdle at Navan (2m, heavy) in November (close up behind useful yardstick Switch From Diesel); didn't quite get home over 2m4f (good) here last time so this shorter trip should suit; tongue-tie goes on; big chance.

RaceName: ITBA Mares Maiden Hurdle
OwnerName: Nicholas Fahy/Mrs Orla Fahy/Two Sheets to the Wind Syndicate
Spotlight: Sent off favourite on debut in a Thurles bumper and found just one too good; failed to build on that when tailed off at Punchestown (reportedly made a respiratory noise) and tongue-tie goes on for hurdles debut after six months off; market can guide.

RaceName: ITBA Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Failed to win in seven point-to-point starts; inauspicious start over hurdles at Punchestown (21.5f, gd-yld) when 33-1, weakening quickly; much more needed dropped in trip.

RaceName: ITBA Mares Maiden Hurdle
OwnerName: Claire Wylie & Mrs Orla Cleary-O'Kane & Mark McGuckin
Spotlight: Placed in a point-to-point; respectable first two runs over hurdles and in a bumper at this venue but may be seen to better effect when tackling handicaps.

RaceName: ITBA Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: 1-6 in point-to-points but has failed to complete on four occasions, including when pulling up the last twice; can only be watched on hurdling debut.

RaceName: ITBA Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-8 over hurdles but has ability and was a good second in a mares' maiden hurdle over C&D (good) on penultimate; not as good upped to 2m6f on latest; the drop back in trip is a plus here but rated 88 so probably place claims at best.

RaceName: ITBA Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Extremely modest form on the Flat on the AW and on turf; can only watch on hurdles debut.

RaceName: ITBA Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Represents a top yard and sent off at just 7-2 on debut in a Leopardstown bumper in March but never landed a blow and was beaten 36l; bred to have a future over jumps and too soon to write off.

RaceName: ITBA Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: No sign of winning potential in three maiden runs at 2m-17.5f; tongue tie now goes on; seems safe to rule out on return from 119 days off.

RaceName: ITBA Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Soundly beaten on debut in a Cork bumper in May; marginally better when beaten 14l in another bumper at Roscommon latest; major improvement required on first start over timber.

RaceName: ITBA Mares Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: 4yo; promise in academy hurdles in the winter on testing ground; modest fourth in 4yo maiden at Roscommon (2m, yld-sft) on return from break last month, earning a rating of 104; half-sister to two good ground winners so could improve for this surface and leading chance.

RaceName: EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Placed at Goodwood and Nottingham (beaten a short head by last week's Group 2 winner Senorita Bonita) before finishing 6.25l 14th in the Queen Mary; sound chance now down in grade; tongue-tie added.

RaceName: EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Home-bred filly by Harry Angel; closely related to 6f 2yo winner Deb's Delight and half-sister to three other winners; dam 6f 2yo winner (including a Listed race); interesting newcomer.

RaceName: EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: 100,000euros yearling by Blackbeard; first foal; dam French Listed-placed 5.5f/6f winner; has no easy task on debut.

RaceName: EBF Fillies' Novice Stakes (GBB/IRE Incentive Race)
Spotlight: Having first run at 5f, but has run two ordinary races so far (6f/7f) and likely to be outclassed.

RaceName: British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 11-2 when making his belated debut in novice at Nottingham (1m2f, good to soft) in May, close up 1m before finishing sixth of ten; trainer won this race in 2018, 2020 and 2021; could be a big improver.

RaceName: British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 62,000euros yearling, 110,000euros breeze-up 2yo; tenth foal; Ghaiyyath half-brother to winners Sir Andrew (7f-1m1f including HK Group 1; RPR 126), Katara (Spanish 1m) and Alejandria (Spanish 7f-1m2f); dam Italian 7f/1m3f winner, out of 7.7f 2yo winner; late foal; the market can guide on debut.

RaceName: British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Slowly away when beaten more than 20l in good-ground maidens at Newbury (1m2f) and Pontefract (1m4f) so he has no form claims.

RaceName: British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
OwnerName: Mrs R F Greener And North Farm Stud
Spotlight: 50-1 for maiden at Sandown (1m2f, good) one month ago and slowly away but he made a big move in the penultimate furlong before running out of steam late on, beaten 3l in fifth of six; likely to prove a good deal better and, although stamina is questionable on pedigree, he's one to take seriously.

RaceName: British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: Fair form when second at Kempton (7f, AW) last October and when beaten just under 6l at Newbury (1m/1m2f, good) on both starts this season; big player.

RaceName: British EBF Novice Stakes (GBB/GBBPlus Race)
Spotlight: 11l second to the subsequent Royal Ascot flop Water To Wine in novice at Kempton (1m3f, AW) on debut in May; weak 11-1 at Doncaster (1m4f, good to soft) 18 days later, however, and readily left behind from over 1f out to finish a well-beaten last of five; mixed messages.

RaceName: Dandara Homes EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 40,000euros yearling and 90,000gns breeze-up buy; brother to German 7f winner Elli Pirelli, closely related to German Group-placed 8.5f 2yo/1m1f winner El Loco (RPR 104); market support would heighten interest on debut.

RaceName: Dandara Homes EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Beaten four times but placed in his first three races and was beaten under 4l when ninth of 21 in the Coventry at Royal Ascot (6f, good to firm); he'll be difficult to beat with a repeat of that fine effort; cheekpieces added.

RaceName: Dandara Homes EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has finished last in both his races and handicaps will be more like it.

RaceName: Dandara Homes EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 215,000euros breeze-up buy; showed ability when third in a four-runner field at Carlisle (5f, good to firm); he was the only colt against fillies that day; has to improve plenty if he's to trouble Jaan Ki Tukri.

RaceName: Dandara Homes EBF Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 150,000gns foal, 210,000gns yearling; by No Nay Never; second foal; dam Group-placed 6f winner (RPR 98), half-sister to useful 6f 2yo/7f winner Elinor Dashwood; has to be of interest.

RaceName: Randox (C & G) Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Three-time Flat winner; 0-9 as a hurdler but a repeat of his third in a Tramore handicap two runs back shouldn't see him far away; beaten about 11l in another handicap here last time.

RaceName: Randox (C & G) Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: AW winner whose five defeats over hurdles include a laboured handicap debut in March.

RaceName: Randox (C & G) Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Has looked limited in going 0-5 in points and makes no obvious appeal on rules debut.

RaceName: Randox (C & G) Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Weak 16-1 chance when an early casualty at Roscommon last week; Jack Kennedy rides the yard's other one.

RaceName: Randox (C & G) Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: British point form nothing to shout about, nor his run in an Aintree bumper; stable/hurdle debut.

RaceName: Randox (C & G) Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Ability in bumpers and ran okay to be a 13l fourth in a 2m3f maiden here a month ago when tongue tied for the first time; each-way claims.

RaceName: Randox (C & G) Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: A maiden under both codes and now exposed over hurdles, being 0-14; however, he's finished runner-up on seven occasions and, although below his useful best in 2026, he has the strongest form claims in this weak a maiden.

RaceName: Randox (C & G) Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Three runs in Flat maidens for Donnacha O'Brien; best when 6l second over a near-1m2f trip on heavy at Gowran in September; 20-1, jumped moderately and beaten 20l on hurdle debut at Punchestown in December.

RaceName: Randox (C & G) Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: 40-1 when tailed off on yielding to soft ground at Roscommon a month ago.

RaceName: Young Guns Handicap
OwnerName: Mrs Polly Gibson & Miss Pollyanne Gibson
Spotlight: Two siblings won Listed and Group 2 sprints; clear evidence of ability at York (5f, good to firm) last May, behind rivals who are way above this bracket; off for 11 months after and he didn't show much on his return at Doncaster in April but his AW run since (first-time cheekpieces, retained) was better; no surprise if he goes well on handicap debut.

RaceName: Young Guns Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden after 12 starts but he has gone close in two C&D handicaps (good to firm) this year and to his credit that he nearly got back up three weeks ago, in this headgear combination; in the thick of it.

RaceName: Young Guns Handicap
Spotlight: Early promise for previous yard but didn't progress and it's been a struggle off high marks since switched to handicaps; dropping steadily in the weights and down in grade so may do better; has worn a hood, first-time cheekpieces today.

RaceName: Young Guns Handicap
Spotlight: Opening mark suffered from one good run as 2yo and although she's dropped a few pounds this year she didn't find as much as the second Jack Rabbit Slims and third Power Of Chora over C&D recently; shade more needed.

RaceName: Young Guns Handicap
Spotlight: Failed to progress as a 2yo and was well held in sprint handicaps on the AW and turf for previous yard; however, he shaped nicely on his debut for this one when third at Catterick (6f, good to firm) in June so now he's of more interest again.

RaceName: Young Guns Handicap
OwnerName: Richard Fahey Ebor Racing Club Ltd
Spotlight: Has found her level in 5f handicaps of late, getting off the mark in quite taking style at Ripon (good; chief victim has won since) before having a troubled run at Musselburgh recently; also has form on a stiff track and should be able to plunder something else off her current mark.

RaceName: Young Guns Handicap
Spotlight: Fairly routine form in her first handicaps but she is more favourably treated now and the drop to 5f seemed to suit when coming from off the pace to finish close up behind Jack Rabbit Slims over C&D (good to firm; in this headgear combination); can take a hand again.

RaceName: Young Guns Handicap
Spotlight: Improved form when second on handicap debut at Catterick but she played up before the start of a C&D race since (joint-favourite; Jack Rabbit Slims second) and failed to come up with much; now has something to prove.

RaceName: Nelson Restaurant For Classic Raceday Dining Handicap
Spotlight: One win from 14 races; three seconds from five starts this year, beaten a head at Newmarket (2m, good to firm; made most) 18 days ago in first-time cheekpieces; they are now replaced by blinkers; last time he raced over shorter than 1m6f was in June 2025 but this isn't a strong race; good chance.

RaceName: Nelson Restaurant For Classic Raceday Dining Handicap
Spotlight: Beaten about 3l into fourth of five at Windsor (11.4f, good to firm) on her second start but well beaten on starts either side; lacks solid achievements but this is her handicap debut, so early days.

RaceName: Nelson Restaurant For Classic Raceday Dining Handicap
Spotlight: Nine wins over the years, including two early last summer, but he's been going the wrong way this term; admittedly badly hampered on latest outing; 6lb below his last winning mark.

RaceName: Nelson Restaurant For Classic Raceday Dining Handicap
Spotlight: Bagged a hat-trick last summer, including his second C&D win; back to his last winning mark and second at Chepstow (1m4f, good to firm) suggested on penultimate start that he is almost as good as ever; big shout.

RaceName: Nelson Restaurant For Classic Raceday Dining Handicap
Spotlight: Runner-up on stable debut at Southwell (1m3f, AW) in February but unable to get competitive since; latest start was a rare turf race and (poor form in this sphere) and overall score is 1-19.

RaceName: Nelson Restaurant For Classic Raceday Dining Handicap
Spotlight: Gained sole win here last June and beaten a neck over C&D late last month; front-running with first-time blinkers (retained today) at Carlisle five days ago did not work out but he needs a second look on return to Leicester.

RaceName: Golwg Gwendraeth Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: His standout run is the close third at Windsor (5f, good to firm) and on that performance this mark looks realistic.

RaceName: Golwg Gwendraeth Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: First two runs were at Ascot and Goodwood; dropped back to 5f when third at Leicester last time (good to firm), running on well in the closing stages; should have a major say here on nursery debut.

RaceName: Golwg Gwendraeth Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Well down the field in all his races, in cheekpieces in final two starts; has to step up to defy this mark.

RaceName: Golwg Gwendraeth Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Useful pedigree but no closer than 7l in three races over the minimum distance and this mark doesn't look obviously lenient, though Taryn Langley takes off a handy 5lb.

RaceName: Golwg Gwendraeth Nursery Handicap
Spotlight: Hasn't shown a lot but handicapped accordingly and a tongue-tie now goes on; market helpful.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: C&D winner in mares' handicap hurdle in May off 8lb lower; good run in novice hurdle over C&D on penultimate; another solid effort in a Wexford handicap chase on latest; goes well here and should be on the premises again.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Nigel O'Hare & Mark Devlin & Philip Smith
Spotlight: Much improved second of 14 over C&D last month; was still beaten 10l, however, so place chance but needs to find a bit more to win off the same mark.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Three-time winner; veteran last scored over 3m at Perth last August; signs of life on latest at Wexford but this trip may be a touch sharp.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won at Cork last July off 1lb lower; hasn't really clicked into top gear yet this term and well beaten on latest but handicapper is at least giving her a chance so can't be totally discounted.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-9 over hurdles; two runs this year haven't suggested a trip to the winner's enclosure is imminent; others appeal more.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Three-time Flat winner is 0-5 over hurdles; far from disgraced on debut for this yard at Wexford 11 days ago (2m1f, good); most of best form is on soft ground, however, so could probably do with some rain.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Sole success came off 4lb lower at Kilbeggan in April 2025 (2m2f, gd-yld); soundly beaten in two runs since returning from a year off; needs to get back on track.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Ordinary form in a trio of maidens including two at this venue; not obviously well treated for her handicap debut but related to winners so could improve; worth a market check.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-15 over hurdles but runner up on three occasions, including twice over C&D; sent off at short enough odds here the last twice but failed to deliver; place claims if produces best.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Just a glimmer of promise in six starts over hurdles; modest form in point-to-points on last three starts; tongue tie goes on for return to hurdling for new yard; likely best watched.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: 0-7 over hurdles; hasn't really threatened in first three starts in handicaps; beaten 32l at Listowel on latest; eased 3lb but hard to make a case for on all known form.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Maiden was placed on a few occasions when trained by Tony Martin; modest first two runs for this stable; beaten 45l on latest; cheekpieces now reached for.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won twice at Clonmel last spring on heavy (2m) and good (18.5f); is just 1lb higher now than for the latter win and this trip more suitable than when soundly beaten over 2m6f here last time.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Maiden was pulled up when last seen over hurdles in September 2024; similar story in three point-to-points earlier this year; new yard now and tongue tie goes on; watch.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Now With A Free Trial Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Two wins on the Flat but 0-15 over hurdles; didn't run badly in this sphere at Navan in November but pulled up the next day at Fairyhouse and fell at Tramore on return from break latest; others more persuasive.

RaceName: Malcolm Greenslade Doncaster LVA Stalwart Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Best of his three runs when neck second to a useful prospect at Hamilton last time (1m1f, good to firm); interesting on handicap debut.

RaceName: Malcolm Greenslade Doncaster LVA Stalwart Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Won a 7f Thirsk maiden last July (good); hasn't progressed since going handicapping and was beaten nearly 5l over 1m at Thirsk last time (good); may improve at 1m2f.

RaceName: Malcolm Greenslade Doncaster LVA Stalwart Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: AW maiden winner in February (8.5f); placed in decent handicaps at York (1m2f, good) and Epsom (1m2f, soft) on last two starts; has made the running; chance now down in grade.

RaceName: Malcolm Greenslade Doncaster LVA Stalwart Memorial Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark in an 8.5f maiden here last time (good; third has won since); weakened when tried over 9.5f on AW as a 2yo but may yet improve over this sort of trip (dam won over 1m2f).

RaceName: Join Racing TV Today Handicap
Spotlight: Two bits of modest form in his five races; last of 11 when 40-1 for last month's handicap debut (6f, soft) seemed to confirm that there are more solid options but, in fairness, it was his first race for 640 days.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Today Handicap
Spotlight: Off 19 months before this season; her second race back (seventh attempt overall) was her best when off the mark at 25-1 in a classified at Southwell (5f, AW) and in quite taking style too, so she needs a second look despite her handicap mark having been revised upwards in a major way.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Today Handicap
Spotlight: Modest maiden after six starts (five for Jack Channon) but she was prominent for a long way under Rose Dawes when fourth of 12 at Newbury (good to firm) five days ago and that was clearly her best show from three handicaps; first attempt at 5f for this drop back in class might also offer some hope.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Today Handicap
OwnerName: T Beeches, C Stone, J Gallagher
Spotlight: Wore this equipment combination on last three starts, beaten a nose in a Brighton handicap (5.2f, good to firm) on first occasion and making all in a classified here (5f, good) on second; wide draw and interference leaving the stalls prevented those tactics at Windsor three weeks ago; 5lb claimer is on board this time; should be of renewed interest.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Today Handicap
Spotlight: Switched to handicaps when fourth of nine at Brighton (5.2f, good to firm) in May in a first-time visor; no show there (7f) last Tuesday and the visor is now removed; needs a marked overall step forward.

RaceName: Join Racing TV Today Handicap
Spotlight: 2-30 overall, with the second win in a classified at Bath (5.7f, good) early last month; ran moderately under the same 7lb claimer at Brighton two weeks ago and may need his best run since last May.

RaceName: Diplomat Hotel Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Finished a remote fifth over 7f at Chepstow (good to firm) and limited appeal after that; drops back 2f.

RaceName: Diplomat Hotel Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Mrs Wendy Jarrett & J S Moore
Spotlight: A belated debut for this gelding who is out of a 5f winner and sister to Listed-placed 7f/1m winner King's Field; likely best watched.

RaceName: Diplomat Hotel Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Becoming exposed after six defeats and two of those were at odds-on, including the latest at Brighton; consistent though and won't be far away provided the new visor doesn't trip him up.

RaceName: Diplomat Hotel Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: RPRs have regressed in each of her runs and she has no obvious form claims here; had wind surgery and now tongue tied.

RaceName: Diplomat Hotel Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Int Plywood (lmporters) Ltd And C Mason
Spotlight: Half-sister to 5f AW/6f turf winner Outer Edge (RPR 67); dam 5f-6f winner (80); unlikely to provide the answer to this on her debut.

RaceName: Diplomat Hotel Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Had lots of chances now (0-10) and although she's regularly knocked on the door, there have been off days and Pontefract last time was one of those; others preferred.

RaceName: Diplomat Hotel Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 80,000gns yearling; went off 28-1 at Southwell (6f AW) but saw her race out well to be third and back in fourth was last week's Newmarket handicap winner Planet Seeker; dropping back to 5f wouldn't be an obvious move, but she should have every chance with improvement surely on the cards.

RaceName: Plus 2 Print Remembering Tony Oakes MBE Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Didn't show much in two points and can only be watched on rules debut.

RaceName: Plus 2 Print Remembering Tony Oakes MBE Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: 2m4f point winner; ran in these colours when based in Britain, running his best race when third in a Cheltenham bumper in October 2023; later beaten 8l and 12l in two runs over hurdles; back from a long absence but retains some potential on debut for Willie Mullins.

RaceName: Plus 2 Print Remembering Tony Oakes MBE Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Beaten single-figure lengths in two bumpers at Kilbeggan so looks up to winning races, and the dam won over hurdles.

RaceName: Plus 2 Print Remembering Tony Oakes MBE Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Only 1-16 in points and now 0-13 under rules, including 11 defeats over fences; opposable.

RaceName: Plus 2 Print Remembering Tony Oakes MBE Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Latest run in a Punchestown handicap needs forgiving but runner-up in five of his 11 races over hurdles and brings the best form into this maiden.

RaceName: Plus 2 Print Remembering Tony Oakes MBE Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Dead-heated in a point but that was a modest race; headgear applied for rules debut; best watched.

RaceName: Plus 2 Print Remembering Tony Oakes MBE Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Point winner who is 0-5 over hurdles and has a mark of 91; shouldn't be good enough unless transformed by cheekpieces.

RaceName: Plus 2 Print Remembering Tony Oakes MBE Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Best run was here when beaten a little under 8l in a bumper this May; modest since, including on hurdle debut.

RaceName: Plus 2 Print Remembering Tony Oakes MBE Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: 200-1 when beaten a long way in a 2m4f maiden at Limerick.

RaceName: Plus 2 Print Remembering Tony Oakes MBE Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Half-sister to 3m hurdle/3m1f chase winner Winnie Woodnutt; dam unraced half-sister to winners Vics Canvas (bumper and useful 2m4f-3m4f hurdle/chase), Art Lord (2m-3m hurdle/chase) and Horse Force One (bumper/2m2f hurdle); likely one to watch on debut.

RaceName: Plus 2 Print Remembering Tony Oakes MBE Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Three non-completions in points and beaten knocking on a distance on hurdle debut.

RaceName: Plus 2 Print Remembering Tony Oakes MBE Maiden Hurdle
Spotlight: Half-sister to two winning hurdlers and dam an unraced half-sister to high-class bumper and 2m-2m4f hurdle/chase winner Racing Demon; a 4yo filly with all the allowances.

RaceName: Beverley Annual Badgeholders Handicap
Spotlight: 12-race maiden; creditable third to a pair of subsequent winners at Carlisle last time (1m1f, good); conditions suit; should go well.

RaceName: Beverley Annual Badgeholders Handicap
Spotlight: Has won six races here, from 7.5f to 1m4f, the latest last August (1m4f, good to firm); creditable third here in May (8.5f, good) but below-par last two runs; could go well.

RaceName: Beverley Annual Badgeholders Handicap
Spotlight: Four-time course winner at up to 8.5f, latest success being in April (8.5f, good to firm); fair form since including when 2l fourth here last time (7.5f, good to firm); tries this trip for first time.

RaceName: Beverley Annual Badgeholders Handicap
Spotlight: Hasn't won for more than two years, but sound effort when length second over C&D in May (good to firm); each-way chance.

RaceName: Beverley Annual Badgeholders Handicap
Spotlight: Two wins last season, the first a dead-heat over C&D in July (good to firm); rather patchy form this season, best run when third at Pontefract in April (1m, good); a possible on second run back after wind op.

RaceName: Beverley Annual Badgeholders Handicap
Spotlight: Progressive 3yo; has done well since tried in an eyeshield with wins over this trip at Leicester (good) and Wetherby (good to firm); 4lb higher; one to consider.

RaceName: Beverley Annual Badgeholders Handicap
Spotlight: Unexposed 3yo; ordinary form over 7f; likely to improve significantly up in trip (dam a Group-placed 1m2f winner) and should go well on handicap debut.

RaceName: Beverley Annual Badgeholders Handicap
Spotlight: Won over 1m2f at Newcastle in March, and creditable third over 8.5f here last time (good); each-way chance

RaceName: Beverley Annual Badgeholders Handicap
Spotlight: 18-race maiden; second over 8.5f here last month, but most of her form this term is fairly ordinary; others appeal more.

RaceName: Evening Racing @leicesterraces Wednesday 22nd July Handicap
Spotlight: Off the mark at Wolverhampton (7f, AW) in May and second in two of his last three starts; latest at Chepstow (7f, good to firm) six days ago was just a six-runner race but it looks like he will be bang there again.

RaceName: Evening Racing @leicesterraces Wednesday 22nd July Handicap
Spotlight: Front-runner on last two outings; not proven on firmer than good after three attempts, two before she went handicapping and the other on reappearance; it's therefore possible to forgive those and, after two wins (7f) and two seconds from her last five starts, she needs major respect.

RaceName: Evening Racing @leicesterraces Wednesday 22nd July Handicap
Spotlight: Ran poorly at Redcar on latest start but went into that in good form, including for a narrow Redcar win; all ten races have been 6f but he looks worth a go at 7f; one to consider even though he may need to bounce back with some improved form.

RaceName: Evening Racing @leicesterraces Wednesday 22nd July Handicap
Spotlight: Modest form in her three Irish races and no better in the two in Britain, although she has been more competitive with the switch to handicaps (5.7f/6f); needs to build on that with today's step back up in trip.

RaceName: Evening Racing @leicesterraces Wednesday 22nd July Handicap
Spotlight: Made her mark in 6f/7f Brighton nurseries last season but she's hardly beaten a rival this term; blinkers go on.

RaceName: Evening Racing @leicesterraces Wednesday 22nd July Handicap
Spotlight: Low-level maiden after seven races, which has still resulted in minor honours in three handicaps over 1m/7f before her form dipped over 6f on latest start; even best form needs improving on.

RaceName: Preventapest Handicap
Spotlight: Both turf wins have come at Doncaster where he beat only one home last time out (7f, good); that was his fourth disappointing run in succession.

RaceName: Preventapest Handicap
Spotlight: 6f novice winner who is 0-4 in handicaps but was bang there in a Class 4 at York a month ago (7f, good) and has major claims with a repeat of that.

RaceName: Preventapest Handicap
Spotlight: He was 1-16 when winning a weak novice at Chelmsford in November (7f, AW); hasn't kicked on from there and yet to get overly competitive in handicaps.

RaceName: Preventapest Handicap
Spotlight: Both wins for Michael Dods were on ground softer than good; picked up for 1,000gns and ran quite well on his stable debut over C&D as he was quite fresh after a break; possibilities if building on that.

RaceName: Preventapest Handicap
Spotlight: No wins in eight; had placed form for Edward Bethell but signed off for that yard under a cloud; would have claims if he can turn a corner for new yard.

RaceName: Preventapest Handicap
Spotlight: Two AW wins for this yard last year but has yet to look as effective on turf (0-6); well beaten on comeback at Lingfield (7f, good).

RaceName: Preventapest Handicap
Spotlight: Exposed six-time winner who has performed moderately in her last two races over 1m; on the plus side, she hasn't had many chances at 7f over which she was a close fourth on her comeback.

RaceName: Preventapest Handicap
Spotlight: Eight-time winner who has slipped to a good mark but last season didn't end well and this campaign has yet to get going; a 9yo now.

RaceName: Preventapest Handicap
Spotlight: Has won on the AW and heavy ground (here); only fourth of eight here two weeks ago and that was a Class 6.

RaceName: Go fibrefast With Fibrus Broadband Rated Novice Hurdle
Spotlight: Leopardstown maiden hurdle winner (2m, soft) in March and runner-up to the smart Switch From Diesel in a novice two runs back; only seventh in a handicap since then but was beaten far; every chance on these terms.

RaceName: Go fibrefast With Fibrus Broadband Rated Novice Hurdle
Spotlight: Won a point and added a 3m maiden at Punchestown in May (good), which is very much his standout performance under rules; has since disappointed at Wexford but in first-time blinkers that are swiftly replaced by cheekpieces; difficult to know what to expect.

RaceName: Go fibrefast With Fibrus Broadband Rated Novice Hurdle
Spotlight: Well held in two bumpers and she beat a limited 250-1 chance by 2l in a C&D maiden; not sure that entitles her to a mark of 109, but she's a half-sister to the smart Fun Fun Fun, so could have more to offer.

RaceName: Go fibrefast With Fibrus Broadband Rated Novice Hurdle
Spotlight: Hooded for the first time when beating 12 rivals in a 2m6f maiden at Tramore (good) and got pestered up front when beaten 5l in a 2m2f novice here a month ago; should give his running and might not be far away back up in distance.

RaceName: Go fibrefast With Fibrus Broadband Rated Novice Hurdle
Spotlight: Dual Flat winner who won a C&D handicap off 92 here in May; similar performance when runner-up on last month's return visit; looks vulnerable in a race of this nature.

RaceName: Go fibrefast With Fibrus Broadband Rated Novice Hurdle
Spotlight: Limited maiden who has failed to complete her last three races; now blinkered.

RaceName: Connexin Gigabit Gallop Handicap
Spotlight: Dual C&D winner last season (soft/good to firm); not been in quite such good form this time, but is on a good mark as a result; wide draw not ideal.

RaceName: Connexin Gigabit Gallop Handicap
Spotlight: Has won six times on the AW, but 0-15 on turf; creditable second at Wetherby two runs back (5.5f, good to firm) but well beaten last time (6f).

RaceName: Connexin Gigabit Gallop Handicap
Spotlight: 5f Southwell winner in April, but last of 12 at Hamilton on latest start (5f, good); bit to prove.

RaceName: Connexin Gigabit Gallop Handicap
Spotlight: Four wins over 5f/6f last season; back to winning ways at Thirsk last time (6f, good to firm; cheekpieces back on); not ideally drawn but a contender off 4lb higher.

RaceName: Connexin Gigabit Gallop Handicap
Spotlight: 3lb lower than when winning at Catterick in April (5f, good); mainly disappointing since, although was only 2l behind Barmyblade (5lb worse off) when third at Thirsk last time (6f, good to firm); well drawn and a possible.

RaceName: Connexin Gigabit Gallop Handicap
Spotlight: Has run two good races this season, last time getting off the mark on turf at Wetherby (5.5f, good to firm); each-way chance.

RaceName: Connexin Gigabit Gallop Handicap
Spotlight: 2-39 on turf, but creditable fourth at Ayr last week (6f, good); worth considering.

RaceName: Connexin Gigabit Gallop Handicap
Spotlight: Ex-Ewan Whillans; sound run on stable debut when second at Wetherby last time (5.5f, good to firm); each-way chance.

RaceName: Connexin Gigabit Gallop Handicap
Spotlight: Winner at Newcastle last November, but disappointing since; blinkers now tried (well beaten in a visor last time); plenty to prove at present.

RaceName: Connexin Gigabit Gallop Handicap
Spotlight: 22-race maiden; third in a Leicester classified two runs ago (cheekpieces on first time) but well beaten last time.

RaceName: Leicester Racecourse Ideal Conference Venue Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Has a 3-25 record on AW, including 9.4f wins this February and June; she's had only four races on turf and they were in 2024, showing some ability but not on a par with today's almost career-high mark.

RaceName: Leicester Racecourse Ideal Conference Venue Fillies' Handicap
OwnerName: Michael P Tudor & J P M O'Connor
Spotlight: Fair maiden who has taken minor honours in two of her four handicaps, a rallying second of eight at Ripon (1m2f, good) on latest outing; unraced on firmer than good; firmly in the mix if she acts on it.

RaceName: Leicester Racecourse Ideal Conference Venue Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Switched to turf for last three starts and best of those when winning under Tom Kiely-Marshall over C&D (good to firm) on latest, albeit in a three-runner race; back up 3lb but considered seriously in another small field.

RaceName: Leicester Racecourse Ideal Conference Venue Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Similar form on all four starts, latest her handicap debut at Windsor (11.4f, good to firm) when weakening into fourth of five; tries 1m2f for the first time and needs to find a bit extra.

RaceName: Leicester Racecourse Ideal Conference Venue Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Won two in a row on AW in March and continued in good form on next two starts; considered on that form but beaten 20l on last two outings, on turf debut (1m2f, good) last time.

RaceName: Dandara, Beautiful New Homes In Carway "Confined" Handicap (Not Won A Flat Race In 2026)
Spotlight: The neck defeat on good to soft at Bath (1m) offers hope but lesser efforts have followed; needs one of her good days.

RaceName: Dandara, Beautiful New Homes In Carway "Confined" Handicap (Not Won A Flat Race In 2026)
Spotlight: Keeps missing the break but he still went close two starts ago at Chepstow and it wasn't a bad run here last time, again over 1m; returning to further looks a good move.

RaceName: Dandara, Beautiful New Homes In Carway "Confined" Handicap (Not Won A Flat Race In 2026)
Spotlight: Behind Racing Demon last time but was only 4l away over 1m and his sole win was at this trip; can run well.

RaceName: Dandara, Beautiful New Homes In Carway "Confined" Handicap (Not Won A Flat Race In 2026)
OwnerName: Mrs R F Greener And North Farm Stud
Spotlight: Lightly raced but was slowly away on last month's handicap debut (1m, good) and never counted; work to do with Racing Demon and Homeland on that running.

RaceName: Dandara, Beautiful New Homes In Carway "Confined" Handicap (Not Won A Flat Race In 2026)
OwnerName: Mrs Muriel Forward & Dr G C Forward
Spotlight: On a good mark and beaten a neck at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm) two runs back, form that has worked out well; poor last time over an extended 1m3f but that trip seems too far.

RaceName: Dandara, Beautiful New Homes In Carway "Confined" Handicap (Not Won A Flat Race In 2026)
Spotlight: Has regressed since a half-promising debut and was nowhere in a classified last time.

RaceName: Dandara, Beautiful New Homes In Carway "Confined" Handicap (Not Won A Flat Race In 2026)
Spotlight: Modest maiden over jumps and hasn't performed well in 7.5f-1m2f non-handicaps on the Flat.

RaceName: Dandara, Beautiful New Homes In Carway "Confined" Handicap (Not Won A Flat Race In 2026)
Spotlight: Better for going handicapping when beaten 4l into third at Chepstow (1m2f, good to firm) and she was keeping on; one of the more appealing options.

RaceName: Dandara, Beautiful New Homes In Carway "Confined" Handicap (Not Won A Flat Race In 2026)
Spotlight: Tailed off in her first handicap but in a first-time visor she only just failed to get up in a 1m2f classified race; if the headgear works again then she should be competitive.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Three wins last season and shaped nicely on return to hurdling at Bellewstown (24.5f, good) 10 days ago; never won off a mark this high but definite place shout dropped back in trip.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: In excellent form in 2026; beaten a head at Ayr in March (2m, soft); won easily over this C&D (good) early last month; denied by a neck at Cartmel (2m6f, good) on latest; 12lb higher now than when successful here but can give another good account.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Two wins in 2023 for Gordon Elliott on soft and heavy ground; no joy since but step in the right direction in a handicap chase here on latest (19.5f, good); needs to back that up now back over hurdles.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Has won twice this season, both at this venue; scored over C&D (soft) in March off 18lb lower; went in again over 2m2f here a month ago; raised 7lb for that and has never won off a mark this high but can't be discounted in current form.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Glimpses of ability in maiden hurdles; only midfield on handicap bow at Leopardstown in December; off since but eased 2lb and has run well fresh before so worth a market check.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Handicap Hurdle
OwnerName: Mrs T Giles-Fahey & Miss S Mangan
Spotlight: In excellent form and has won two of her last three, both over C&D; is now 17lb higher than when winning easily here in May and this is stronger than the race she won last time but clearly loves this place and in the mix again.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Two-time Flat winner; makes handicap debut over hurdles after not showing a whole lot in qualifying runs; potential improver and needs checking in the market for shrewd yard.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Five-time Flat winner is just 1-6 over hurdles; running just okay on the level recently and has stamina to prove for this trip now back over timber; others appeal more.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won a novice handicap over 17.5f (yielding) here in October; subsequently out of depth in grade 3 and tailed off on return from a break in a handicap at Perth in May; unproven over this far and needs to leave that last run behind.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Has returned from six months off in fine fettle; won at Wexford (2m4f, gd-yld) off 96 and beaten a neck off revised mark of 102 at Punchestown (23.5f, good); go well again off just 1lb higher.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Won twice last season, over 19.5f (good) at Clonmel and over 23.5f at Tipperary; well held on return from 306 days off at Punchestown but should strip fitter now, eased 1lb and steps back up in trip; can't discount.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Minor promise in maidens; well beaten on handicap debut at Navan (3m1f, yld-sft) when last seen in December 2024; worth checking in the market on return from long absence.

RaceName: Tote Guarantee, Never Beaten By SP Handicap Hurdle
Spotlight: Point-to-point winner; not beaten far on handicap debut at Wincanton in October 2024; pulled up when last seen at Southwell in April 2025; has joined top yard; market can guide.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Fillies' Handicap
OwnerName: Hasmonean Racing, C Fahy And Marchants
Spotlight: Made all for clearcut success on handicap debut here (9.4f) in January and came second on both subsequent AW starts (9.4f/1m4f); absent since lesser effort on turf two months ago and her tendency to race too freely is a nagging concern but this return to Tapeta is sure to suit and she remains unexposed over the trip.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Gained all three wins at around 1m on turf; yet to hit top gear after three runs turf this season (1m-1m1f) and moves up in trip with a quite bit to prove.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Eight-race maiden; left Roger Varian after disappointing run in the autumn and was well beaten on both starts for new stable this spring (1m2f/8.3f; had wind op in between); back up in trip with cheekpieces tried here; big leap of faith required to support her.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Lighty raced for her age and made quite promising seasonal debut when third at Lingfield (1m2f) in April but ought to have been well suited by the good to soft turf when below that form at Ffos Las (1m4f; favourite) last month and has a point to prove here.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Evidently well suited by soft turf when displaying improvement to win 1m2f Ffos Las maiden by nearly 5l last month, her third start; on a different surface here but could still have more to offer over the new trip.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Unexposed 3yo who displayed clear promise on all three 2yo starts (1m-8.6f); not obviously well treated for this seasonal/handicap debut but has plenty of scope for improvement over 1m4f.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Won in good style on last season's reappearance outing (1m2f, good) but was not in the same form on any of her five subsequent runs last year and was safely held when back in action in April; off for another 75 days since and not yet proven over this far; needs to get career back on track.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Safely held when fourth of five in tactical C&D contest on handicap debut in March and not seen since; that defeat is not veiwed that harshly and she may still have potential but others appeal more all the same.

RaceName: Sky Sports Racing Sky 415 Fillies' Handicap
Spotlight: Narrowly won 1m4f Southwell novice in April, her second start; yet to build on that performance in two subsequent handicaps but may not have been suited by making her own running on first occasion and would also have preferred a stronger overall pace when third at Kempton last time; has a bigger run in her if/when there is more emphasis on stamina.

RaceName: Racing Again On Monday Evening Handicap
Spotlight: Tough type who has been on the go since last December, winning at Nottingham and just touched off at Leicester (both at about 1m), but seemingly suited by the shortest trip he's tackled this year when making all over C&D 11 days ago; back up 3lb; can go well again.

RaceName: Racing Again On Monday Evening Handicap
Spotlight: Won 6f maiden on Newcastle AW as 2yo for previous yard but has found it a mighty struggle since, without and with headgear (first-time visor today); has much to prove over this stiff test and needs more weight off back.

RaceName: Racing Again On Monday Evening Handicap
Spotlight: Good run of form in May and June once hooded, finishing runner-up in two 7f handicaps before getting off the mark at Wetherby (1m, good to firm); ran out of steam after failing to settle at Nottingham (about 1m) 12 days ago but this test, in a less competitive race, should suit better.

RaceName: Racing Again On Monday Evening Handicap
OwnerName: Buist, Shields, Thompson And Mrs R Carr
Spotlight: Three wins in 2025 included two over C&D (good to firm); this is a belated return by his standards but he has won after a break and would be very competitive if reasonably straight for his comeback.

RaceName: Racing Again On Monday Evening Handicap
Spotlight: Unraced as 2yo; useful start when a close third of six at Redcar (6f) in April but less convincing since and her effort petered out when upped to 7f at Musselburgh on handicap debut; still early days but work to do for now.

RaceName: Racing Again On Monday Evening Handicap
Spotlight: Faded over C&D on his yard debut following a break and has been kept to 6f since, improving for the fitting of cheekpieces when winning at Ripon (good) in May; in nothing like the same form in two starts since, though.

RaceName: Racing Again On Monday Evening Handicap
OwnerName: Ursa Major Racing Ltd & Julie Camacho
Spotlight: Well held in two AW runs, including on handicap debut at Newcastle (7f) 19 days ago but she had run quite promisingly, despite being beaten about 7l, on her reappearance at Thirsk last month and ought to have some potential in ordinary handicaps.

RaceName: Racing Again On Monday Evening Handicap
Spotlight: All races at 5f and he hasn't achieved much in either handicap, for previous yard and this one; some encouragement in pedigree for a step up in trip but probably best watched even so.

RaceName: Private Boxes For Best View @leicesterraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: 0-21; latest of his three seconds was in February in first-time cheekpieces; slipped leaving the stalls on latest start; has since left Joey Ramsden; being visored first time didn't spark anything on penultimate outing but now it's the turn of blinkers and he has one of the better chances on ratings.

RaceName: Private Boxes For Best View @leicesterraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Low-level form in her six runs in Ireland for Donal Commins; off 17 months before well held in a 5f classified on AW last month; best watched.

RaceName: Private Boxes For Best View @leicesterraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Sole win (12 races) was in a 6f AW classified in February; switched to turf (good to firm) for his last two outings and needs to produce much better than those efforts.

RaceName: Private Boxes For Best View @leicesterraces Classified Stakes
OwnerName: J May, R L Field, W Trenchard, J Pook
Spotlight: Sprung a 50-1 surprise in a handicap at Lingfield (5f, AW) in January, for his only win from 21 races, but right out of form since.

RaceName: Private Boxes For Best View @leicesterraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: 23-race maiden when she turned up for a handicap at Chepstow (6f, good to firm) 17 days ago but she won that seven-runner race, always prominent; that was a clear career best and it's striking that it was her first race at short of 6f; big player if the form is backed up.

RaceName: Private Boxes For Best View @leicesterraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: 9yo who has had 16 wins on AW, in January most recently; sole turf win (from 19 attempts) was at Yarmouth in 2022 but he came within a neck of another in a classified at same track (6f, good to firm) 18 days ago, which was the first time this year that he's worn his usual cheekpieces; has a shout if that form is repeated.

RaceName: Private Boxes For Best View @leicesterraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Modest maiden at his best in 2024 and 2025; not so good for new yard this year but latest start, when wearing first-time cheekpieces over 7f, was at least a step back in the right direction.

RaceName: Private Boxes For Best View @leicesterraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Low-level form after six starts, which was nevertheless good enough for minor honours on good ground in two of her three handicaps (6f/5f) and the exception was on soft; unraced on firmer than good; best form running on over 5f on latest start and headgear is now enlisted; needs improvement but given a second look.

RaceName: Private Boxes For Best View @leicesterraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Sole 2yo run was for Karl Burke; cut little ice that day or in her two recent outings; big drop in class and a visor is given a go, but no form claims.

RaceName: Private Boxes For Best View @leicesterraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Sole win from 12 starts came at Lingfield (5f, AW) in January on penultimate outing for James Owen; comparable form in two of six turf races since for new yard and not totally disgraced on latest, so he's not entirely ruled out despite not being proven over quite this far.

RaceName: Private Boxes For Best View @leicesterraces Classified Stakes
Spotlight: Chelmsford (6f, AW) in December was his only win from 17 races; three minor honours on good ground this spring, before lesser shows on last two outings; place chance if back to form.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
OwnerName: Mrs Paul Shanahan & Mrs Wendy O'Leary
Spotlight: Closely related to Australian Group 2 winner Sixties Groove, half-sister to Flat/hurdle winner Smiling Eliza; shaped quite well when fifth behind year-younger rivals on belated debut in a 7f maiden at the Curragh in March; ground was heavy then. may be better suited by a sound surface.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Tenth foal; Harzand filly is a sister to minor 1m2f winner Harseva, half-sister to four winners, including prolific AW scorer Aramis Grey; dam unraced half-sister to high-class miler Intikab; stable in magnificent form with four winners last Saturday

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Smart maiden for this stable at two, made the frame on all four starts, notably when third in the Goffs Million and fourth (demoted from third) in a 1m fillies' Group 3 event; placed in three of her four starts for Brendan Walsh in the US; has returned to Joseph O'Brien, who fit a tongue-tie; some element of risk attached to her eight-race maiden status, but hard to oppose.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Favourite for her debut in a 17-runner maiden at the Curragh last September, broke slowly, recovered to challenge before fading late; questions to answer after a disappointing run at Leopardstown in October; heavy ground may have been against her then.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Half-sister to Group 3 winner over 1m6f Raise You and champion bumper performer A Dream To Share; showed some ability for the Kiely stable last season; perhaps more likely a handicap prospect over a bit further.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Ran well in a 1m4f Limerick claimer; well beaten in two maidens since then; takes a sharp drop in distance now; would receive 25lb from Celtic Motif in a handicap.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Maiden
Spotlight: Ran twice at Dundalk last November; will need to get handicap mark.

RaceName: Dandara, Find Your Perfect New Home Handicap
Spotlight: Has been running well over hurdles and this dual AW winner hasn't had many Flat chances on turf (0-3); on a competitive mark and Hugh Horgan takes off 5lb.

RaceName: Dandara, Find Your Perfect New Home Handicap
Spotlight: On a good mark and knows how to win; however, three of his four runs since a break have been underwhelming.

RaceName: Dandara, Find Your Perfect New Home Handicap
OwnerName: Spiers & Hartwell Ltd & Mrs E Evans
Spotlight: Five-time winner; first run since March when a rallying third over C&D three weeks ago; thereabouts.

RaceName: Dandara, Find Your Perfect New Home Handicap
OwnerName: Lady Bamford , Mrs Collins & Bv Sangster
Spotlight: Cost 290,000gns in 2024; edged out Grand Pier with the pair dominant on her handicap debut (1m2f, soft) and then nearly defied a 3lb rise at Nottingham (good to firm); up another 2lb but she'll surely be in the mix, provided she lasts home over this longer trip.

RaceName: Dandara, Find Your Perfect New Home Handicap
Spotlight: Signs of improvement when sixth at Newbury (1m2f, good) and duly built on that with a near miss behind Liveinthelight here over 1m2f (soft), pulling clear of the third; should be fine over this far.

RaceName: Downpatrick Racecourse Family Funday Supporters INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Second in his first point and held every chance when falling late on in the second; latest effort wasn't so clever but now embarks on rules career with Gordon Elliott.

RaceName: Downpatrick Racecourse Family Funday Supporters INH Flat Race
Spotlight: No worthwhile form in a point, two maidens and latterly in a bumper; cheekpieces fitted.

RaceName: Downpatrick Racecourse Family Funday Supporters INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Tailed off in two maidens at Thurles but has since been switched to Emmet Mullins; market informative.

RaceName: Downpatrick Racecourse Family Funday Supporters INH Flat Race
Spotlight: No show in a couple of points and readily opposed here.

RaceName: Downpatrick Racecourse Family Funday Supporters INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Half-brother to useful 2m hurdle/chase winner Pisgah Pike; dam 2m3f hurdle winner (also placed 1m2f-1m4f Flat; RPR 62); he wouldn't be the obvious answer on debut.

RaceName: Downpatrick Racecourse Family Funday Supporters INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Finished a close fourth at Kilbeggan (good to yielding) despite finding a bit of bother in running; can improve.

RaceName: Downpatrick Racecourse Family Funday Supporters INH Flat Race
OwnerName: Mrs Gemma Casey & Ms Bernadette Leigh
Spotlight: Half-brother to five winners including Royal Kahala (bumper/smart 2m3f-3m hurdle), Collectors Item (useful 2m-3m2f hurdle/chase) and Robbie Rabbit (point/3m hurdle); unraced dam out of top hurdler Lady Rebecca; worth a market check.

RaceName: Downpatrick Racecourse Family Funday Supporters INH Flat Race
Spotlight: 28-1 when beaten nearly 20l in a Punchestown bumper and will need to leave that behind.

RaceName: Downpatrick Racecourse Family Funday Supporters INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Purchased for 20,000euros since finishing runner-up in two 3m points and should have a future for her leading stable.

RaceName: Downpatrick Racecourse Family Funday Supporters INH Flat Race
Spotlight: Dam useful 2m3f-3m hurdle/chase winner, half-sister to useful winners Mister Hyde (2m4f-3m hurdle/chase) and Glenquest (2m-3m hurdle/chase); a 4yo filly with the allowances and from a respected yard.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Has struggled in each of his seven starts over hurdles (1m7f-2m3f) and has a tricky draw to contend with on his Flat debut; best watched.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: A'Ali gelding; fourth foal; half-brother to 7.5f/1m2f winner Princess Niyla (RPR 76); dam 9.4f/1m2f AW winner (99), out of unraced half-sister to Group-placed 1m2f winner Tiffany Diamond and 1m2f/1m4f winner Nizamar; market should be informative on his debut.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Showed up well for a fair way before fading into sixth on his debut at Doncaster (7f, good to firm) last month but proved disappointing at Chepstow (7f, good to firm) 11 days ago; drops in trip and makes his AW debut (out of a Listed winner on the AW) with better needed.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Failed to beat a rival home on his debut at Bath (5.5f, firm) last year but stepped forward from that when a 3l second of eight at Yarmouth (6f, soft; winner has won again since) last month; that was his first start for ten months and he's shortlisted on his AW debut.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Showed little when last of eight on his debut at Lingfield (6f, good; 66-1) last month and now switches to the AW with a big step forward needed.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 26,000gns yearling; fifth foal; half-sister to winners Messerschmitt (1m 2yo, 90; useful 2m hurdle) and Goose Rock (5f-1m2f incdluding AW; 67); dam 7f/7.4f 2yo winner (88), out of half-sister to 1m Group 1 winner Nahoodh; needs a close look on her debut.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
OwnerName: Ursa Major Racing & Alfa Site Services
Spotlight: Missed the break and looked in need of the experience on her debut here over 5f four weeks ago, beating only one rival home; steps up in trip and she's open to improvement.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Looked green on her debut at Bath (5.5f, good) last month but kept on well to finish fifth off 11 (beaten around 3l); wasn't able to build on that at Southwell (7f, AW) nine days ago but this half-sister to seven winners remains capable of better.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Ran well to a point on her debut at Lingfield (6f, good) last month but went backwards from that effort when beating only one rival home at Nottingham (5f, good to firm) ten days ago; likely one for handicaps after one more run.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Looked a difficult ride (pulled hard and hung left) on her debut at Yarmouth (7f, good to soft) last month and again hung left when keeping on into third at Brighton (6f, good to firm) three weeks ago; clearly has ability, but may not be the easiest and stall 12 makes things difficult.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Mayson filly; first foal; dam unraced half-sister to winners Lethimfly (5f 2yo) and Infinity Blue (6f), out of 7f 2yo winning half-sister to HK 1m Group 1 winner Xtension; interesting newcomer.

RaceName: Free Tips On attheraces.com Restricted Maiden Stakes (Bands C And D) (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Ran green when fifth of six on her debut at Kempton (5f, AW; 28-1) last April when trained by Mark Usher, failing to settle and hanging left, and has been absent since; best watched unless the market speaks favourably on her yard debut.

RaceName: Hotel Killarney Maiden
OwnerName: Mrs Suzanne McAuley & Ms C A Jennings
Spotlight: Winning hurdler; 0-11 on the Flat, second in a Clonmel maiden last season; best of four runs this term when a staying-on third in a C&D maiden; down the field in a Leopardstown handicap; made a lot of the running before fading into fourth in a maiden over an extended 1m1f at Ballinrobe.

RaceName: Hotel Killarney Maiden
OwnerName: Sheikh Khalid Bin Ali Al Khalifa
Spotlight: Second foal; gelded son of Make Believe is a brother to Ollie Sangster-trained 1m AW winner Suspicion (RPR 85); dam unraced half-sister to 6f Listed winner Ninjago.

RaceName: Hotel Killarney Maiden
Spotlight: Low-rated hurdler; moderate sixth of nine on Flat debut in a 1m5f maiden at Down Royal.

RaceName: Hotel Killarney Maiden
Spotlight: Has a useful Flat pedigree but no sign of ability; tailed off in an academy hurdle last December; dismal run in bumper at this venue in May; held by Coul Dreamer on Ballinrobe running.

RaceName: Hotel Killarney Maiden
Spotlight: Fair form in maidens at Gowran and Limerick before a disappointing AW run; failed to run up to his handicap mark in an auction maiden at Ballinrobe last time, visored now.

RaceName: Hotel Killarney Maiden
Spotlight: First seven runs were on AW, initially for Andrew Balding; placed in three of five Dundalk outings for this stable, including a 3l third over 1m in March; made little impression at Limerick on turf debut; better when fourth over C&D in May; closely matched with Could Dreamer on that running.

RaceName: Hotel Killarney Maiden
Spotlight: Concerning that he has failed to build on his debut second behind two Aidan O'Brien-trained colts in a 7f juvenile maiden at the Curragh; ran only twice last season, and his best effort was when second at Gowran on the first occasion; same pattern this season, fair second at Dundalk before falling to improve at Gowran, perhaps a tongue-tie will make the difference

RaceName: Hotel Killarney Maiden
Spotlight: Fairyhouse third (7f, yielding) last September her best effort; unplaced favourite on heavy ground at Galway on her final start last season; official rating of 73 is high enough to put her in the mix in a maiden of this standard.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap
OwnerName: Jim And Claire Ltd And Jon & Kay Collins
Spotlight: Bettered disappointing seasonal debut when fifth of seven at Goodwood (6f, soft) last month and is now back on same mark as when dead-heating at 80-1 at Chelmsford in the autumn but others have more convincing profiles.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap
Spotlight: Low-mileage 4yo who won twice over 5f on Tapeta (once here) in the autumn and was placed over that trip on handicap debut at Newcastle in January; not quite in same form on turf last month but that run came after a break and he could quite plausibly have untapped potential over 6f back on AW.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap
Spotlight: Belied 50-1 odds when going close at Kempton (6f; AW debut) in May, his second start for Mark Loughnane, but was quickly back in the doldrums (albeit on good to soft ground) on next outing; hard to predict.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap
Spotlight: Looked as good as ever when close third of 16 on seasonal debut at Ripon (6f, good to soft) in April but his form has dipped since and he has questions to answer on this AW debut.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap
Spotlight: Managed only fifth of eight at Kempton last month and is now 0-6 for current stable; continues to slip down the weights but his tendency to race too freey remains a concern; swaps cheekpieces for first-time blinkers.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap
Spotlight: Five AW wins, the latest from the front off 2lb lower at Newcastle (6f) in February; well below that form on turf lately but still worth keeping an eye on in the betting.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap
Spotlight: 2-4 over 7f for this stable last year but made a low-key return over that trip in May and is not sure to be suited by this drop back to 6f.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap
Spotlight: Showed career-best form when winning at Lingfield (6f, soft) two starts ago and is also suited by this C&D but could be a shade vulnerable off a mark this high.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap
Spotlight: Dual 6f Southwell novice winner this spring and again ran well when keeping-on second on last month's handicap debut at Kempton; could still have more to offer back on Tapeta; high on the list.

RaceName: attheraces.com/marketmovers Handicap
Spotlight: Came good at seventh attempt, making all for clearcut success in C&D novice six weeks ago; contests deeper race on this handicap return but is evidently in good nick; no surprise if he features.

RaceName: Irish Examiner Handicap
Spotlight: Narrowly denied on nursery debut at the Curragh last September (7f, yld-sft); again ran well when a close-up fourth at Dundalk in October; flopped on heavy ground on reappearance; struggled again on good ground at Roscommon; questions to answer.

RaceName: Irish Examiner Handicap
Spotlight: Showed ability at two with a third placing in a 7f Down Royal nursery; saddle slipped on her reappearance at Cork; mot beaten far in fifth over a shorter trip at Limerick on Saturday; place possibility with Oisin Murphy up.

RaceName: Irish Examiner Handicap
Spotlight: Two less encouraging runs since starting the season brightly with a fourth placing at Roscommon; testing ground probably the issue at Leopardstown; less easy to overlook his Bellewstown failure behind Cause I Like You, Stonyfalls and Beau Army then.

RaceName: Irish Examiner Handicap
Spotlight: Not a bad run from a difficult draw at Leopardstown in her first handicap; possibly capable of better.

RaceName: Irish Examiner Handicap
Spotlight: Showed some ability on AW over 5f-6f during the winter; failed to beat a single rival in his two races after being gelded; took a step in the right direction at Limerick on Saturday, though Zheleznaya Dama was in front of him then.

RaceName: Irish Examiner Handicap
Spotlight: First of her three runs was her best, form well-advertised by the winner Bobby McGee; down the field at Ballinrobe in her first handicap; a new headgear combination tried.

RaceName: Irish Examiner Handicap
Spotlight: Ordinary form in AW handicaps for Gavin Cromwell, much the same at Naas on stable debut; trainer brought off a superb four-timer last Saturday.

RaceName: Irish Examiner Handicap
Spotlight: Failed to make much impact at Naas on handicap debut; hard to make a case for her, unless this shorter trip suits better.

RaceName: Irish Examiner Handicap
Spotlight: Modest form in maidens, paid the price for racing too keenly in a 1m1f handicap at Leopardstown last time.

RaceName: Irish Examiner Handicap
Spotlight: Good run at 50-1 when third over 1m on handicap debut at Dundalk in March; has failed to build on that; gelded since his last run.

RaceName: Irish Examiner Handicap
Spotlight: Winner over 6f/7f at Dundalk; ran her best race on turf when fourth behind the progressive Meriden three starts ago; needs to reverse recent Bellewstown form with Cause I Like You and Stonyfalls.

RaceName: Irish Examiner Handicap
Spotlight: Has slipped to a basement mark and has begun to show winning potential; stayed on well for second behind an easy winner at Bellewstown; faces a rematch with third-placed Stonyfalls, as well as Beau Army and Wicklow Gap; holds a leading chance.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 130,000gns (breeze-up) 2yo; third foal; half-brother to winners Sun Never Sets (Group-placed 7.7f-1m3f; RPR 106) and Proletariat (US Graded stakes-placed 5f/6f turf including 2yo); dam Spanish 1m/US 1m1f winner (103); interesting pedigree, but it's unnerving to spot headgear on a newcomer.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Out of a half-sister to triple 5f Group 1 winner Bradsell but always behind when 50-1 for debut at Windsor (6f, good to firm) a fortnight ago; remains best watched.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Flopped when warm favourite for Brighton debut in May and showed only minor promise when sixth of seven at Ffos Las (also 6f) a fortnight ago; up in trip for AW debut; improvement required.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Out of a French Listed-placed 7f winner; bettered low-key debut when respectable third of six at Pontefract (6f, good) last month and has possibilities if he makes any further progress over this longer trip.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Well down the field on both starts (6f/7f); needs to take a big step forward.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: £62,000 (breeze-up) 2yo; raced too freely and finished last of nine when 16-1 for recent debut at Lingfield (7f, AW).

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 175,000euros (breeze-up) 2yo; third foal; dam unplaced 1m2f turf/1m3f AW (RPR 51), closely related to 1m Listed winner Mooharib, half-sister to 1m2f Listed winner Trethias, out of maiden half-sister to 1m2f/1m4f Group 1 winner Dar Re Mi; with respected stable; no surprise if he features on debut.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Out of a useful and well-connected 1m 2yo winner; made promising debut when keeping on from the rear for fourth of ten at Newbury (6f, good) in May and subsequent odds-on defeat at Catterick (7f; hampered) can be excused; sets the form standard here and still has significant potential.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 14th foal; sister to 7f AW winner Beta Reader (RPR 66), half-sister to four winners including Bedale (1m2f; 107), Big Meeting (1m/1m2f AW; 100) and Ex Gratia (6f AW; 84); dam 5f and 5.5f (Listed) 2yo winner (102), half-sister to Arc winner Bago; well worth a glance in the betting.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: 525,000gns yearling; first foal; dam ran twice at 7f (RPR 47), half-sister to winners Royal Champion (KSA 10.5f Group 1) and Outbox (1m4f Group 2), out of 1m4f Group 2 winning half-sister to 7f/1m Group 1 winner Dubawi; with that price-tag and pedigree, she's a newcomer to take very seriously.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Fourth foal; dam 1m-1m4f winner (including 2yo/Group 1; RPR 117), half-sister to winners Midas Touch (1m2f Group 2; runner-up in St Leger and Irish Derby) and Streetcar To Stars (useful 1m2f); stable has only occasional first-time-out 2yo winners but this filly has an excellent pedigree.

RaceName: Download The At The Races App Maiden Stakes (GBB Race)
Spotlight: Out of a half-sister to an Australian 1m6f Group 2 winner; never looked dangerous when midfield in 13-runner Newbury maiden (6f, good to firm) 12 days ago but that still goes down as an encouraging debut and she can improve over this longer trip.

RaceName: Cygames Prix de Malleret (Group 2) (Fillies) (Turf)
Spotlight: Won a 1m1f110yd Listed event at Bordeaux Le Bouscat in May on good to soft, but was behind Behrayna and Zlata here over 1m3f on very soft before that; took a major step forward last time, though, when finishing a short-neck runner-up in the Prix de Diane (1m2f110yds, good to firm); anything like that sort of level of performance would make her a big player back up in trip, with Pierre-Charles Boudot on for the first time.

RaceName: Cygames Prix de Malleret (Group 2) (Fillies) (Turf)
Spotlight: Yet to be beaten and stepped up in trip on every run since her debut in April, beating Zlata the last two times, most recently in a 1m4f Group 3 at Chantilly on good ground; also in front of Pink Panthera before than when winning a 1m3f Listed event here; sure to have more to come and has strong claims.

RaceName: Cygames Prix de Malleret (Group 2) (Fillies) (Turf)
Spotlight: Debut fifth has been followed by three wins, the most recent her career best when landing a 1m2f Listed event here on good to soft, when Proxima Du Centaur was fourth; needs to step up again but unexposed and should improve; first time Colin Keane rides.

RaceName: Cygames Prix de Malleret (Group 2) (Fillies) (Turf)
Spotlight: Finished over 4l behind Behrayna two runs back when fourth of six and then a shade over 5l behind Dispatches 37 days ago, both races coming here; Ryan Moore an interesting jockey booking on first at over 1m4f but others far easier to make cases for.

RaceName: Cygames Prix de Malleret (Group 2) (Fillies) (Turf)
Spotlight: Won on her debut over a mile on soft in September and shown steady progression this campaign, although she has been about 2l behind Behrayna on her two most recent runs; stays this trip fine but will need to up her game a bit to get the better of the Francis Graffard filly this time.

RaceName: Killarney Plaza Hotel & Spa Handicap
Spotlight: Two wins in 2025, a 7f claiming maiden at Roscommon (good) and a Naas handicap (7f, good); a long way below his best on seasonal debut at Leopardstown in April.

RaceName: Killarney Plaza Hotel & Spa Handicap
Spotlight: Four wins to his name, including a C&D maiden success in 2021; last win was in 2023; fair form last year; no encouragement from this term's four starts

RaceName: Killarney Plaza Hotel & Spa Handicap
Spotlight: Got off the mark in a Gowran handicap over this trip in May and was a close second at Ballinrobe next time; doubts raised by his two most recent outings; beaten favourite for the Curragh race in which No More Porter was second

RaceName: Killarney Plaza Hotel & Spa Handicap
Spotlight: All three wins have been gained at the Curragh; eight of his 17 places have also come at that venue, including a fine second there on his latest start; not guaranteed to produce his best form, though he managed a second placing here in 2023 on his only visit.

RaceName: Killarney Plaza Hotel & Spa Handicap
Spotlight: C&D maiden winner on yielding to soft a year ago; handicap form in unconvincing; in rear at Limerick on seasonal debut in April.

RaceName: Killarney Plaza Hotel & Spa Handicap
Spotlight: Second to a front-running winner in a weak 7f Roscommon contest was his best run last season;  absent since a poor AW effort on handicap debut last November.

RaceName: Killarney Plaza Hotel & Spa Handicap
Spotlight: Fair form in maidens last year; disappointing on handicap/seasonal debut at Leopardstown; poor run at Roscommon over an extended 1m2f; better when dropped to 7f at Fairyhouse 13 days ago.

RaceName: Killarney Plaza Hotel & Spa Handicap
Spotlight: Not one of her better efforts at the Curragh last time; hard to win with; best run this season when second in a six-runner contest; others preferred.

RaceName: Killarney Plaza Hotel & Spa Handicap
Spotlight: Showed enough in three AW maidens last winter to suggest he could pick up a race; tongue-tie and cheekpieces on for turf/seasonal/handicap debut.

RaceName: Killarney Plaza Hotel & Spa Handicap
Spotlight: Sole win came in a 7f Brighton maiden in 2024; nothing of note in his form since finishing third of seven at Limerick 12 months ago; in rear in this year's edition of that race on Saturday; has been struggling in handicaps.

RaceName: Killarney Plaza Hotel & Spa Handicap
Spotlight: Has a better strike rate on AW (7-24); however, capable enough on turf (3-24, placed four times) to have a say in a race like this. best of three starts on turf this year when second over 1m at Limerick in April.

RaceName: Killarney Plaza Hotel & Spa Handicap
Spotlight: 1m1f Leopardstown winner last year for his previous yard; recent seconds at Gowran and Naas have been over longer trips than this; holds a definite chance all the same; Oisin Murphy booked.

RaceName: Killarney Plaza Hotel & Spa Handicap
Spotlight: Maiden produced back-to-back solid runs at Ballinrobe and the Curragh earlier this season; has failed to reach the same standard on last three starts, the most recent in a claimer; held by Mythical Rock on Limerick running

RaceName: Killarney Plaza Hotel & Spa Handicap
Spotlight: Winner on AW in January; unlucky not to finish closer when sixth at Gowran, short of room when travelling well; backed that up with a promising second at Limerick; failed to make his presence felt when a well-supported favourite at Gowran two days later;  the race may have come too soon.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Has won on turf but better on the AW and may have needed it at Wetherby last time after a break (and wind surgery); perhaps more effective at 6f, though.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Only seventh over C&D last time but she's usually reliable here and was a winner in March; nothing wrong with her mark.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div I)
