Spotlight: Dual winner for Roger Varian (7f/1m; AW/good to firm); has slipped to a good mark and he halted the slide in his fortunes with a third at Yarmouth; considered despite an awkward draw.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Current wellbeing is an issue as he's performed poorly since his win in January; best known for sprinting exploits.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Three-time AW winner over 6f; yet to offer much over this far (0-5) and hasn't beaten a rival in his last two races.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Won over 1m at Southwell in October (1m AW; 5lb lower) but not many positives to draw on since that win; off since January.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Both his wins have come on AW, the latest here in June off 5lb lower; current mark will be no excuse and a greater concern would be his last run at Musselburgh.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: No wins in 23 but has been banging at the door in recent months and won't mind returning to 7f.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Three-time winner on the AW in 2025; back in good form after three competitive runs over 7f and quite appealing.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div I)
Spotlight: Close third at Dundalk (5f, Polytrack) in January on final Irish start; his debut for this one was promising but well beaten since; now 1-18.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div I)
OwnerName: The Limes Of Epsom Racing Club
Spotlight: Not well drawn for one who likes to race prominently and last two runs have been underwhelming; others safer.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
OwnerName: P J O'Brien & Mrs M V Magnier & Mrs P Shanahan & K Blake
Spotlight: Dual winner in France with plenty of cut in the ground. First run here for Joseph O'Brien after nine months off; stable could not be in better form, but a watching brief may be the best policy.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Four-time winner over 1m with ease in the ground on all occasions, including at this meeting last year; handles good ground too; has never tackled a trip this far, but just failed over 1m1f at Gowran and every chance that she will get it; big player if she does.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
OwnerName: Bronsan Racing & H Redknapp & John Breslin
Spotlight: Did just about enough to land a Dundalk maiden in January and did similar when following up in a 1m2f handicap at Leopardstown; a little bit more exposed on her two subsequent handicap efforts under a small penalty, but has the ability to get involved here.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Showed promise on her Leopardstown debut and followed up with a battling success over 1m1f on easier ground at Gowran; handicapper has not taken many chances with her mark, but there might be more to come over this trip and the homecoming champion jockey is booked.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Dual winner last year for Richard Hannon; bought to go hurdling but little to recommend in that discipline; best watched on her return to the level.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Two wins over hurdles for Joseph O'Brien and her sole Flat win was in a Tramore maiden over 1m4f last summer; first run since October having changed stables; market will guide but likely to come on for this.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Soft ground winner on debut over 1m2f at Leopardstown in June; overcame some adversity and showed a good attitude; better ground here, but a promising filly and the handicapper looks to have given her a chance.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Landed a Dundalk maiden early in the year; has found life a little tougher in handicaps since and was out of her depth in Listed company last time; others preferred here.

RaceName: Irish Stallion Farms EBF Fillies Handicap
Spotlight: Good servant to her connections; four-time winner from 7f to 1m2f, but very much at home on much softeer ground than she meets today; up against it taking on some promising fillies with younger legs today.

RaceName: Cygames Grand Prix de Paris (Group 1) (No Geldings) (Turf)
Spotlight: Arc entry who is unbeaten in three starts and turned in best effort when taking the Group 3 Hocquart over 1m3f at this venue in May; this will likely be the quickest ground he's tackled and more needed again in grade but he's a fascinating runner.

RaceName: Cygames Grand Prix de Paris (Group 1) (No Geldings) (Turf)
Spotlight: Mostly progressive; followed Group 3 win at Saint-Cloud with a creditable fifth to subsequent Eclipse winner Constitution River in the Jockey Club; shapes as though the step up to this trip will be in his favour - backed up by pedigree - and he'll likely give a good account.

RaceName: Cygames Grand Prix de Paris (Group 1) (No Geldings) (Turf)
Spotlight: Well beaten on soft ground in the Derby but that run flanked by Listed win at Newmarket and a fine second to Causeway (pair a long way clear) in the Group 2 King Edward VII at Royal Ascot; bit more needed but will likely get the run of the race and could go well.

RaceName: Cygames Grand Prix de Paris (Group 1) (No Geldings) (Turf)
Spotlight: Record is one of steady improvement and he turned in his best effort when winning a Group 3 over this trip on quick ground at Chantilly last time; Andre Fabre has won this a record 14 times but more needed to win going up to this grade for the first time.

RaceName: Cygames Grand Prix de Paris (Group 1) (No Geldings) (Turf)
OwnerName: Michael Tabor & Derrick Smith & Mrs John Magnier
Spotlight: Fourth to Derby winner Christmas Day on his debut but has won his other five starts, with his best effort in refitted cheekpieces when beating Ancient Egypt in a Group 2 at Royal Ascot; decent claims for a trainer who is bidding to win this a sixth time.

RaceName: Cygames Grand Prix de Paris (Group 1) (No Geldings) (Turf)
Spotlight: Improving sort whose best effort came when winning the Queen's Vase, form which was franked at Newmarket last week,; down in trip and up in grade, so more needed if he's to take the winning run to five.

RaceName: Cygames Grand Prix de Paris (Group 1) (No Geldings) (Turf)
Spotlight: Typical Haggas improver who followed his narrow win in the Lingfield Derby Trial with a fine second to Christmas Day in a soft-ground Derby; no issues with the return to a sounder surface and been given plenty of time to get over those exertions; big chance.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: 6f Polytrack winner; on the slide in the handicap for some time; drops again in grade for this first start at the track; has shown he acts on Tapeta at Newcastle and is on a very handy mark in relation to the form he showed there last October; blinkers go on; remains unproven over this trip.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: 19-race maiden who wasn't too far off her best when under 2l second over C&D latest; appears to lack gears at the business end of her races but that recent form suggests she'll be an each-way player.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Arrives seeking a hat-trick after 7f turf wins at Musselburgh (good) and Catterick (good to firm), the latter with ease and his best effort for almost two years; still well treated on this first AW start since March and a repeat of either of his last two efforts would make him tough to beat.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: 1m turf winner (good) who arrives with a bit to prove over this trip; drops 1lb after his latest 4l fourth at Yarmouth (1m, good to soft); unplaced from six attempts on AW surfaces; others make more appeal.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: 1m turf winner (soft/good to firm) who is 1-16 on AW (won over 1m on Polytrack); unexposed over 7f but to have a serious chance he'll need to raise the bar based on his last four starts.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: Moderate form in her three qualifying starts but improved on entering this sphere at Chepstow (6f, good) latest, when she raced prominently but didn't quite have the pace late on; the step up in trip looks positive for her first start on Tapeta; drops 2lb; not ruled out.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: C&D winner when 7lb lower last December; reasonably consistent in five starts since scoring at Lingfield; tongue-tie now tried; each-way player but looks in need of a favour from the handicapper before winning again.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: C&D winner who is without success in over four years; went close over C&D in March when 3lb higher but needs to bounce back after being well beaten (6f, good to soft; first-time blinkers) at Chepstow latest.

RaceName: Free Bets On attheraces.com Handicap (Div II)
Spotlight: 7f turf winner (good to firm) when with Charles Hills; 0-9 for current trainer but his last two efforts have offered more encouragement; each-way player.

RaceName: Rose Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Premier handicap winner over 1m2f at Dundalk last season; upped in trip this year to decent effect; good third in the Chester Plate in May; made no impact in the Northumberland Plate last time but will appreciate the calmer waters here.

RaceName: Rose Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Three-time winner on the AW for Adrian Murray; second in a Dundalk claimer for his current yard on his penultimate start; reportedly burst over hurdles at Down Royal last time; others more likely.

RaceName: Rose Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Dual winner for Charlie Johnston; fair effort on his stable debut in a maiden hurdle here in May; booking of Billy Lee suggests better is expected today.

RaceName: Rose Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Undergoing very much a new lease of life, winning three of his last four, and a ride of 17lb could still be manageable; unproven over this trip but in the form of his life and will be popular with Oisin Murphy booked.

RaceName: Rose Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Three wins on the Flat and two over hurdles for this versatile performer; most recently landing a 2m4f handicap hurdle at Gowran in October; ground versatile in the past although might just prefer it a bit softer these days.

RaceName: Rose Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Looked a horse of some promise early last season but has failed to progress; coming down the handicap but stepping up to this trip has not improved his record.

RaceName: Rose Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Five-time winner; travels well in her races and returned to the level in grand style when accounting for the very useful Eagle Fang in a QR contest at Leopardstown; looks a big player here.

RaceName: Rose Hotel Handicap
Spotlight: Dual winner on the Flat; ran way too bad to bad to be true at Fairyhouse last time but a repeat of his previous effort over this C&D would give him a definite chance here.

RaceName: Radio FG - Prix Maurice de Nieuil (Group 2) (Turf)
Spotlight: Won the Group 2 Red Sea Turf (1m7f, good to firm) earlier in the year and followed that with some solid efforts until recording a career best in the 1m4f Hardwicke Stakes last month when cheekpieces were tried; going back up in trip will suit and tongue-tie goes on, so only issue is the 3lb he has to give away to all rivals.

RaceName: Radio FG - Prix Maurice de Nieuil (Group 2) (Turf)
Spotlight: Improving stayer who heads into this searching for a five-timer having won a 1m7f Listed race at Saint-Cloud recently on good ground; no telling where his improvement will take him but does need more up against some proven Group performers on first start in this company.

RaceName: Radio FG - Prix Maurice de Nieuil (Group 2) (Turf)
Spotlight: Won a 1m7f Group 3 here last August on very soft, but not added to that win since, although run plenty of good races over a range of distances, albeit nearly 3l behind Sons And Lovers (getting 2lb) in Saudi Arabia in February; going back up in trip should be a positive and will be thereabouts if producing his best.

RaceName: Radio FG - Prix Maurice de Nieuil (Group 2) (Turf)
OwnerName: Qatar Racing Ltd, Ecurie Skymarc Farm & Ecurie Des Monceaux
Spotlight: Displayed plenty of smart form as a 3yo, winning three times, including a 1m4f Listed event, and performing creditably in Group races; shaped okay on only run over a staying trip following a gelding operation over the winter on soft ground and comes into this off a career best, achieved at 1m4f; unexposed at the distance and one of the more interesting ones.

RaceName: Radio FG - Prix Maurice de Nieuil (Group 2) (Turf)
Spotlight: Sent off 33-1 when fifth of six in the race 12 months ago (Double Major third), the only other time Cristian Demuro rode, and run creditably at times since; matched his career best two runs ago but, on balance, has something to find being more exposed than some.

RaceName: Radio FG - Prix Maurice de Nieuil (Group 2) (Turf)
Spotlight: Won this in 2024 and a two-time Group 1 winner over 1m7.5f; generally a reliable performer as a stayer and enjoying another good season, his sixth in the Group 1 Prix Vicomtesse Vigier last time not far off his best, so solid chance if running his race.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap
OwnerName: Dr M Voikhansky & Mrs S Voikhanskaya
Spotlight: Overcame a wide trip when narrowly winning here over 9.5f in April and posted another good effort when third of 11 here last month; has since left Kevin and Lauren Frost and steps back up in trip, which is a concern, on her stable debut.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap
Spotlight: C&D winner last April off a mark 2lb higher than today's; travelled well but didn't see things out as well as the first two when third at Redcar (1m6f, good; first-time visor) last month and has solid claims returned to the AW.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap
Spotlight: Posted an improved effort when getting off the mark at the sixth attempt at Newcastle (1m2f, AW) 18 days ago, beating a subsequent winner; a 4lb rise looks fair and he's open to further progress, albeit he does have his stamina to prove for this longer trip.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap
OwnerName: Mike Harris Racing & Nikki Evans Racing
Spotlight: Four of his six wins have been at this track (three over C&D) but his most recent success came at Bath (1m4f, good) in May; two respectable efforts there since and now has cheekpieces back on for the first time in nearly three years; place claims.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap
Spotlight: Showed the benefit of a wind operation when winning a classified event at Windsor (1m4f, good to soft) last month and backed that up with a good second behind an in-form rival at Ayr (1m5f, soft); acts on the AW and she makes the shortlist on this return to handicap company.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap
Spotlight: Lightly raced filly who stepped up on her previous efforts when second of nine at Chepstow (1m4f, soft) on her handicap debut last month; looks capable of building on that now switched back to the AW.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap
Spotlight: Three-time course winner, the most recent coming last March over this trip when trained by James Owen; 0-6 since joining this stable and he was well beaten in a classified event at Newcastle last month.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap
Spotlight: Sole win from 16 starts came on her handicap debut at Lingfield (1m5f, AW) in December; runner-up on three occasions since, twice over this C&D, but she's been behind Nala The Lioness on her last two starts.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap
Spotlight: Well held in each of her three starts in novice/maiden company (1m/1m2f) but she's well related and may yet do better now stepping up in trip on her handicap debut; makes some appeal.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap
Spotlight: Ex-Irish filly who has been in good form since stepping up in trip, winning at Chepstow (1m2f, good to firm) before following up at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm) ten days ago; up 3lb for that latest success but she leaves the impression this trip will suit and could well complete the hat-trick.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap
Spotlight: Beat just one rival home in each of her two starts on the AW for Ralph Beckett (1m3f/1m4f) and she was beaten 16l when last of five at Thirsk (1m4f, good; wind surgery) on her stable debut four weeks ago; much better needed on her handicap debut.

RaceName: Follow @attheraces On X Handicap
Spotlight: Well beaten in two starts last year at around 1m and it was the same story on her reappearance at Leicester (1m2f, good to firm) in May; dam won over 1m4f and she now steps up in trip for her handicap debut.

RaceName: Thorn Plant Hire (Q.R.) Maiden
Spotlight: Bumper and four-time hurdles winner; unraced under Flat rules and comes here on the back of being pulled up in the Connacht National at Roscommon; best watched.

RaceName: Thorn Plant Hire (Q.R.) Maiden
Spotlight: Recent efforts over hurdles a little underwhelming but earned a mark of 84 after three good runs in Flat maidens last year, including when third in this race; major player if able to run up to that form.

RaceName: Thorn Plant Hire (Q.R.) Maiden
Spotlight: Hunter chase winner at Down Royal in May; well beaten on his first try in a Flat maiden last month; much more needed here.

RaceName: Thorn Plant Hire (Q.R.) Maiden
Spotlight: Has ability; not beaten far in a better contest at Leopardstown last month; each-way possibilities.

RaceName: Thorn Plant Hire (Q.R.) Maiden
Spotlight: A very infrequent visitor the track; third in a Curragh maiden in 2022 but a mark of 51 fairly reflects his chance.

RaceName: Thorn Plant Hire (Q.R.) Maiden
Spotlight: Soundly beaten in a bumper and two maidens; needs to improve a good deal to be a factor here.

RaceName: Thorn Plant Hire (Q.R.) Maiden
Spotlight: Very smart performer; acquitted himself extremely well when second in very valuable handicap hurdles at Aintree and Haydock in the spring; just outpointed by the smart Gaoth Chuil in a similar contest at Leopardstown last month for this rider; the one to beat.

RaceName: Thorn Plant Hire (Q.R.) Maiden
Spotlight: Better chaser than hurdler, as he showed when winning a handicap chase off 120 at Ballinrobe in May; no impact in a 1m4f Tramore maiden since but this longer trip and more conventional track is more likely to suit; could go well.

RaceName: Thorn Plant Hire (Q.R.) Maiden
Spotlight: Bits and pieces of form; third in a Wexford maiden hurdle last summer and not disgraced at Leopardstown last month; more needed here though.

RaceName: Thorn Plant Hire (Q.R.) Maiden
Spotlight: Stayed well to land a Sligo maiden hurdle last summer; enjoys good ground but might find a couple of these too quick.

RaceName: Thorn Plant Hire (Q.R.) Maiden
Spotlight: Bumper winner and very useful hurdler/chaser on his day; lost his way last season, but could well play a hand here if he can put it together.

RaceName: Thorn Plant Hire (Q.R.) Maiden
Spotlight: Dual bumper winner and maiden hurdle winner; lightly raced individual and unlikely they have got to the bottom of him yet; back from a five month break, but dangerous if the market gives the right signals.

RaceName: Thorn Plant Hire (Q.R.) Maiden
OwnerName: P Haughey & P M Haughey & John Ball
Spotlight: Won a Cork novice hurdle and a Wexford beginners chase last summer; good run when fifth in a 1m4f Fairyhouse maiden before falling over fences at Wexford two weeks ago; respected.
